page: Microsoft Azure F32ams_v6 is a memory-intensive virtual machine belonging to the Famsv6 server family. It is powered by an AMD EPYC 9V74 processor utilizing the x86_64 architecture. The instance features 32 dedicated physical cores and 32 vCPUs running at a clock speed of 3.7 GHz, with a 1.0 thread-per-core ratio. It is equipped with 256.0 GB of system memory, providing a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core. The processor includes L1d cache of 32 KB, L2 cache of 1.0 MB, and L3 cache of 32 MB, and supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 (including AVX512F, AVX512_VNNI, and AVX512_BF16) and cryptographic acceleration via AES and SHA_NI.

In benchmark evaluations, the F32ams_v6 demonstrates strong to top-tier performance characteristics. Its single-core CPU performance via stress-ng and multi-core Geekbench scores rank in the top 10% of tested servers. Memory bandwidth for small-block cached reads and large-block uncached writes falls within the top 25%, though large-block uncached read bandwidth is average. The server lacks local storage and bundled public IPv4 addresses, meaning storage requirements must be fulfilled via external Premium Storage. This design prioritizes high-density memory and compute capabilities over local data persistence.

The hardware profile is optimized for workloads requiring substantial memory capacity alongside dedicated compute resources. Passmark database operations and Redis SET benchmarks place the instance in the top 25%, indicating suitability for in-memory databases, caching layers, and data-intensive processing. Additionally, its top-tier performance in small-model LLM prompt processing and strong text-generation metrics support lightweight AI inference tasks. From a cost-efficiency perspective, the F32ams_v6 offers a high density of RAM per dedicated core, making it a resource-efficient option for memory-bound enterprise applications without the overhead of local storage hardware.
description: Microsoft Azure F32ams_v6 is a memory-intensive virtual machine from the Famsv6 family, powered by a 3.7 GHz AMD EPYC 9V74 processor. It features 32 dedicated physical cores, 32 vCPUs, and 256.0 GB of RAM, yielding a high 8.0 GB per core memory ratio. The instance lacks local storage, relying instead on Premium Storage capabilities. In benchmarks, the F32ams_v6 delivers top-tier single-core CPU and multi-core Geekbench performance, alongside strong memory bandwidth and database processing capabilities. It supports advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and cryptographic acceleration. This server is optimized for memory-bound workloads, including in-memory databases, caching layers, software compilation, and lightweight LLM inference tasks.
og_description: Microsoft Azure F32ams_v6 is a memory-intensive virtual machine featuring 32 dedicated AMD EPYC cores, 256 GB RAM, and top-tier single-core performance for databases and memory-bound workloads.

tagline: A memory-intensive virtual machine featuring dedicated AMD EPYC cores and high memory density for demanding database and computing workloads.
bullet_points: 32 dedicated AMD EPYC 9V74 physical cores running at 3.7 GHz
256.0 GB of system memory providing 8.0 GB per core
Top-tier single-core CPU and multi-core Geekbench performance
Support for AVX-512 and cryptographic hardware acceleration
No local storage, requiring external Premium Storage volumes
Optimized for in-memory databases, caching, and lightweight LLM inference
